rationale: X independently surfaces the same crash-risk theme the human reviewer (RuixiangMa) raised about `flash_attn_varlen_func`/`flash_attn_func` being allowed to be None while downstream paths call one unconditionally — and correctly notes the masked-path fix was incomplete. Y explicitly lists this exact code as '[validated] ... Correct', a false reassurance that misses the real latent bug. X also raises a plausible, well-grounded correctness bug (int `version=` args silently no-op'ing against `get_kernel`'s PEP440-string API) that echoes the human back-and-forth about version semantics, whereas Y's findings (duplication, test-path coverage, stale comment, docs fallback wording) are all valid and actionable but orthogonal to what actual reviewers flagged. Both reviews are similarly concrete/actionable with file:line references, but X has materially higher recall against the ground-truth concerns and no false-confidence claims.
